attacking_style: Kruijswijk rarely initiates attacks himself; relies on following wheels and maintaining position during accelerations by others. Struggles to respond to explosive changes in pace, particularly in final 1-2km of steep climbs.
team_tactics: Benefits from Jumbo-Visma's high-tempo climbing trains that create sustained attrition rather than explosive selections. Teammates set brutal pace below summit to whittle down groups, positioning him for final kilometers.
terrain_preference: Performs best on steady, long climbs at controlled tempo where power-to-weight matters more than acceleration. Exposed on steep technical finales and when explosive accelerations occur inside final kilometer of summits.
vulnerability: Cannot follow sharp accelerations from elite climbers (Bernal, Pinot). Fades badly on steep technical finales after strong intermediate splits. Form deteriorates rapidly across consecutive Grand Tours (Tour podium → dropped at Vuelta 3 weeks later).
key_patterns: Drops within 1km of summit when race leaders accelerate explosively (2+ observations)
Strong at intermediate checkpoints but fades in final technical sections (2019 TDF stages)
Relies entirely on team tempo rather than personal attacking ability (multiple Grand Tour stages)
Cannot sustain peak GC form across consecutive Grand Tours; rapid decline evident
Finishes 20-30+ seconds behind when explosive climbers attack in finale (4+ observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2019 showed vulnerability to accelerations despite Tour podium; 2022 Romandie result 30+ seconds behind surprise winners suggests decline in elite GC competitiveness
